summary.intent: Reduce massive disk space usage (100GB+) from Rust build artifacts across multiple git worktrees of the moltis project.
summary.outcome: Added `debug = "line-tables-only"` to the dev profile in Cargo.toml and pushed the change, which should cut target/debug size by 50-70%.

repo: moltis is a Cargo workspace with ~15 crates and hundreds of dependencies, making it especially prone to large target/ directories
Cargo.toml uses spaces for indentation in members/default-members lists — whitespace must be preserved when editing

code.finding: Added `debug = "line-tables-only"` under [profile.dev] to reduce DWARF debug symbol size by ~5-10x while preserving stack traces

workflow: Each git worktree gets its own target/ by default, causing multiplicative disk usage — sharing a single target dir via CARGO_TARGET_DIR or .cargo/config.toml is the primary fix
A shell wrapper function for cargo can auto-set CARGO_TARGET_DIR per repo based on git toplevel, sharing artifacts across all worktrees without per-project config
Rust debug builds are large due to: DWARF symbols (10-50x code size), incremental artifacts, multiple artifact types per crate, proc macros, build scripts, and per-crate test binaries
cargo sweep --time 7 can prune stale artifacts; CARGO_INCREMENTAL=0 avoids incremental overhead for one-off builds

friction: Editing Cargo.toml introduced unintended whitespace changes (spaces to tabs) that had to be reverted before committing to keep the diff clean
open_items: Sharing target/ across worktrees via CARGO_TARGET_DIR or shell wrapper was discussed but not implemented — still each worktree likely has its own target/
cargo sweep or periodic cargo clean not yet automated
sccache for shared compilation cache was mentioned but not set up
